content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 19, 2002

Frederick Lee Christensen Architects
295 Main Street, Suite 300
Salinas, CA 93901

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-02-220

Dear Mr. Christensen:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ As specified on our web site, the role of the Political Reform Act (the "Act") is as follows:

The mission of the FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION is to promote the integrity of representative state and local government in California through fair, impartial interpretation and enforcement of political campaign, lobbying, and conflict of interest laws.

It does not appear from the limited information you forwarded, that you or your attorney are public officials regulated under the purview of the Act. Regulation 18329(b)(3)(D) provides that the Commission may decline to provide advice where the request does not present a question under the Political Reform Act. (See also, Reg. 18329(c)(4)(F), copy enclosed.) Thus, we would not be the agency to respond to your request.

Also enclosed is a pamphlet entitled, "Can I Vote?" which may assist you when determining whether a conflict exists on a particular issue before you or your attorney. Should you have any additional questions, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
JWW:jg

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear in Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

sections.conclusion: It does not appear from the limited information you forwarded, that you or your attorney are public officials regulated under the purview of the Act. Regulation 18329(b)(8)(D) provides that the Commission may decline to provide advice where the request does not present a question under the Political Reform Act. Thus, we would not be the agency to respond to your request.

sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requestor did not appear to be a public official regulated under the Political Reform Act, and the request did not present a question within the Commission's jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no detailed question section. The FPPC response explains why they cannot respond rather than addressing a substantive legal question. Enclosures mentioned but not included in provided text.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice to Frederick Lee Christensen Architects regarding the Political Reform Act, determining that the requestor was not a public official regulated under the Act and the request fell outside the Commission's purview.
